description: The spiritual entities and personifications of natural forces in Turkic mythology and ontology.

concepts.meaning: The goddess of fertility, protector of women and children. Associated with the benevolent aspects of the earth.
concepts.note: Often depicted as a mother figure or a swan.

concepts.meaning: The lord of the underworld and the first human to die. He governs the realm of spirits and shadows.
concepts.note: Often seen as an antagonist but also a necessary part of the cosmic balance.

concepts.meaning: The Water Spirit. Every lake, river, and spring is believed to have its own protective spirit.
concepts.note: Humans must show respect to these spirits to avoid drought or floods.

concepts.meaning: The Spirit of the Road or Fate. He guides travelers and decides the paths of individuals.
concepts.note: Represents the dynamic aspect of fate and motion.
